raw_dir: Emmett W. Bassett and Priscilla Tietjen Bassett_interview_20250704_201849
entry_subject: Emmett W. Bassett and Priscilla Tietjen Bassett

applied_corrections.whisper: "the bus board cop" / "the bus boardcop" (Pass 4 finds in raw at byte ~63900)
the women's International League for Peace and Freedom, which was founded by Jane Adams
applied_corrections.correction: Women's International League for Peace and Freedom (WILPF); founded by Jane Addams

applied_corrections.whisper: the Plaza / "what's the big holiday? Plaza"

pending_context.whisper: Uncle Willie Travis at the Alamo
pending_context.notes: "He went out there and he was killed there. But Uncle Willie, I'm talking about somebody who looked like me… Travis. He was killed at the Alamo"; speaker's family legend that Travis (the canonical Alamo commander) was actually Black; preserved as speaker-originating
"I worked with Schaffield Falls. That was a big dick. No. This was after Columbus"; possibly Sheffield Farms Milk Company (the major NYC-area dairy producer, c. 1880–1960s)

"Deatrous food. Yeah… he would give anything to Jaime"; speaker's recollection of the NYC dairy-industry company that "couldn't hire a Black PhD because the whole top would go off of that building" — canonical company name not yet verified
"they were concerned about some of your friends, that it come to Iowa House"; speaker's reference to an FBI inquiry locus — canonical identification not yet verified

"Bayard Rustin and Stanley Levison" co-founders of *In Friendship*; Norman Thomas (1884–1968) was the American Socialist Party leader; one of the foundational In Friendship 1955 founders along with Rustin / Levison / Baker; cross-reference for `civil_rights_facts.json` candidate entry

"Well, we were, we were living in Bramsew in Brooklyn"; uncertain Whisper rendering of a Brooklyn neighborhood name, likely Brownsville (where the Bassetts later did Ocean Hill–Brownsville organizing)

"along that road, there was what was called a roadhouse in those days. And this roadhouse became rather notorious because they refused to serve blacks"; canonical late-1940s segregated Northampton-Amherst MA roadhouse; canonical name not yet verified
"he brought his cousin up. Her name was Ida Dotson"; speaker-originating; Emmett's family-network teacher who promoted him from 5th to 7th grade at the Henry County VA Black school

pending_context.whisper: the Harlem, uh, Army building / In the Army? Yes
pending_context.notes: "There are speeches in Harlem… But it looked like someone went in the Harlem, uh, Army building. Yeah. In the Army? Yes. In the Army? Yes. Yeah, Army." Canonical Audubon Ballroom at 165th Street and Broadway, Washington Heights — Malcolm X's Organization of Afro-American Unity headquarters and site of his February 21, 1965 assassination; the canonical venue for the "speeches in Harlem" Bassett describes attending three or four times. Whisper compressed "Au-du-bon" → "Ar-my."

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly Erwin Chargaff or another Columbia biochem professor)
pending_context.notes: "he had had other professors from Columbia, a doctor-cabinet. And they were great friends. So I guess I got all of those recommendations." Speaker's reference to a Columbia University biochemistry professor who was Tiselius's friend and wrote Emmett's Uppsala recommendation. Most likely candidate: Erwin Chargaff (1905–2002, Columbia biochemistry, "Chargaff's rules"); could also be David Nachmansohn or other contemporaries. Whisper rendering "cabinet" is implausible; canonical name not yet verified.

pending_context.whisper: Dr. Dubois (in Loving v. Virginia context)
pending_context.candidate_correction: (likely Roy Wilkins, NAACP executive secretary 1955–77, or speaker-confused W.E.B. Du Bois)
pending_context.notes: "Well, I did, I do remember what Dr. Dubois said. And it probably was after the loving, loving decision, loving versus Virginia… And he was a leader in the NAACP. And he made the statement that the NAACP did not support, it did not oppose interracial marriage and did not support interracial marriage." W.E.B. Du Bois (1868–1963) died four years before *Loving v. Virginia* (1967), so the statement attribution is chronologically impossible if Du Bois is meant. More likely: Roy Wilkins, NAACP executive secretary 1955–77, made the formal NAACP post-Loving statement. Preserved as speaker-originating; this is Priscilla's recollection of a remembered NAACP figure.

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly "Paulina" or other family ancestor; speaker-originating)
pending_context.notes: "Paul and Paul and Ned is son, but I don't know much about Paul and Ned, who was my great-great-grandmother." Speaker's reference to a great-great-grandmother by an unclear name; the syntax "Paul and Ned" for a female ancestor suggests Whisper has garbled a single name like "Paulina" or "Polonia." Preserved as speaker-originating family genealogy.

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly Travers or Trevett family of Virginia)
pending_context.notes: "I was told that she came from a very wealthy family of the travers around Richmond"; second-pass rendering "travestists" is a Whisper variant of the same word. Canonical identification of a "wealthy [surname] family of Richmond" not yet verified; preserved at low confidence.

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly RENA or a Bronx housing coalition)
pending_context.notes: "we had several neighborhood groups in our community. Which were well-organized, like Reena. They had group, housing group"; speaker's reference to a 1970s NYC Human Rights Commission-allied housing-organizing group; canonical identification not yet verified.

pending_context.whisper: death rich (biochemistry head at Ohio State)
pending_context.notes: "And death rich, that was biochemistry. It was also combined with health science." Speaker's reference to an Ohio State biochemistry-department head c. mid-1950s; canonical name not yet verified — possibly Dietrich, Detrick, or a similar Germanic surname. Whisper "death rich" is the same misrendering pattern as "Dr. Goog" / "goo" for Gould.

"I talked with another man a week or so before I went up there. And he had told me his name was Jim Mullins. He was a pretty well off black band in the community. And I'd run into a lot of problems. He ran the corn meal"; speaker's reference to a Henry County VA Black businessman who tutored Emmett in voter-registration application-writing c. 1942. Speaker-originating; preserved for the canonical first-person record of pre-1965 Black voter registration in southwest Virginia.

pending_context.whisper: "the Lawyers Guild" (implied via In Friendship coalition partners, not flagged in Pass 1/2)
pending_context.notes: If the transcript mentions "Lawyers Guild" in the In Friendship context, that's the canonical 1937-founded progressive-left lawyers' association where Stanley Levison was active. Need re-read of source to confirm presence. (Verification flag, not a confirmed catch.)

pending_context.whisper: Tillesta / Tillester (Henry County VA furniture family)
pending_context.notes: "he worked for another family named Tillesta, who ran the Furniture Company"; "Pop was a cousin to Tillester's wife." Bassett describes a second white Henry County VA furniture-manufacturer family — adjacent to but distinct from the white Bassett family. Stanley Furniture (in Stanleytown VA, adjacent to Bassett VA in Henry County) was the second major regional manufacturer; Thomas B. Stanley was VA Governor 1954-58 and central to "massive resistance" against Brown. The "Tillesta" -> "Stanley" phonetic transformation (Til-le-sta -> Stan-ley reversed-syllable Whisper artifact) is plausible. Could also be Stuart (Stuart Furniture, adjacent VA). Flag for adversarial review.

pending_context.whisper: "in some position at the medical scho[ol]... Meer. Yeah. I can't. I don't know. We never did figure out how to use that name"
pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — speaker's named Iowa-House-affiliated friend of Priscilla's)
pending_context.notes: The "Iowa House" passage (Pass 1 row 38.38; current FLAGGED-FOR-ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW) actually contains a NEW unflagged uncertainty in the same passage: Priscilla and Emmett try to remember the surname of the woman who'd been to Iowa House and whose husband was a doctor at Ohio State University Hospital. Both speakers say "Meer" then fail to recall. Whisper-rendered name "Meer" is the speaker's own non-recall, not a Whisper error per se. Preserved as speaker-originating; this disambiguates the Iowa House FBI inquiry as being about a specific named female friend, NOT the venue itself. Reinforces that Iowa House is most likely a SPECIFIC RESIDENCE or BOARDING HOUSE the friend stayed at — possibly "Iowa Hall" (an Iowa State Department of Public Welfare facility?) or a YWCA/YMCA-type residence whose name Priscilla cannot recall. Suggests the leading P2T candidate "International House" is LESS likely than previously assessed: Priscilla doesn't recall the name, suggesting it's a less-canonical name.

Speaker says "Tester. Tester. And when I... He came back to the country" — directly after referring to Rustin returning from Africa. "Tester" appears to be a speaker-originating nickname (possibly Bassett's mishearing of a Rustin nickname, or "Bayard" -> "Tester" Whisper rendering, or speaker-self-correcting search). Could also be a different person (a Rustin associate) entirely. Speaker-originating; flag for adversarial review.

pending_context.whisper: "John. Yeah. At Ohio State." (Pass 4 raw at byte ~49986)
pending_context.notes: "they would give me this assistant professor, John. Yeah. At Ohio State." The "John" here is anomalous — possibly a Whisper hallucination for a filler word, or possibly the actual name of the assistant professor position-holder. Low confidence; speaker context doesn't disambiguate. Flag for adversarial review.
